text:
Longhi (ロンギ, Rongi) is a Royal Bodyguard for Prince Tubeppa. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 

Appearance

Longhi has fair hair tied back in a long ponytail behind their back, and gaunt cheeks. As a Royal Bodyguard, she wears a black suit and a tie. 

Personality

Longhi seems to be a careful person, as she agree with her superior Maor that the best way of communicating with Kurapika discreetly is by letter, since signaling in code risks interception. She also appears to be less vocal than Maor. This is shown when she did not intervene to support or contradict Maor when he accused Kurapika of being the mysterious killer. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

5 th Prince Tubeppa sends Maor and Longhi to attend Kurapika's two-week teaching of Nen in Room 1014. The two remember Tubeppa's order to persuade Kurapika within the two weeks they're there for, in order to be her ally even if it means involving Prince Woble. Maor believes that a written letter offering an alliance with Kurapika would be best and Longhi concurs as trying to communicate any other way would pose a high risk of getting intercepted. 

After Loberry's outburst, Longhi witnesses Barrigen be killed by four small white snakes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 Following the murder, Kurapika lets his students decide whether to stay or leave. They all remain and he teaches them his first exercise. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 The following day, Longhi returns to Room 1014 for Kurapika's lecture. Longhi does not intervene when Maor and Satobi accuse Kurapika of being behind the attacks after Myuhan's death. 

On the 7 th day of the voyage, Longhi continues taking part in Kurapika's Nen training. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 Like everyone else, she begins to have doubts about the training, since the students always repeat the same exercise. The following day, Longhi observes Kurapika's Water Divination. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 With Kurapika and Bill behind closed doors, they summon Ladiolus to perform Water Divination in private. Satobi confirms once again with Maor if the latter is fine with allowing them to proceed as they please, to which Tubeppa's bodyguard replies that they have no choice on the matter, as their goal is to learn Nen. Longhi remarks that if Woble's camp really intends to exploit their vulnerability, they are playing right into their hands. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 388 

Right after Maor returns from the main bedroom, he confirms to fellow guard Longhi that everything Kurapika said is true. The two remain in the hall until the other students complete their Water Divination. Once the two reconvene with their Prince, Tubeppa assigns them the task of choosing new students among her staff, if Kurapika should announce a second training course. 

A Special Request and A Contract

At 11:45 a.m. on the 10 th day of the voyage, Kurapika and Bill invite Longhi into Room 1014's master bedroom in order to privately initiate her with Nen, as they did with her unawakened classmates. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 Furykov watches her enter, still believing her to be behind the attacks on Room 1014 that killed Barrigen and Myuhan, and loosely hopes she would take Woble out in a kamikaze attack. Longhi steps towards Bill next to a table with equipment to conduct a water divination test, and Kurapika joins them after shutting the door. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 

Instead of waiting for Kurapika and Bill to begin, Longhi volunteers that she can already use Nen, and so does not need divination, surprising Kurapika and Bill who begin to react to her statement. Aware of the implications, Longhi states that she was not the owner of the snakes that killed Barrigen and Myuhan, and proves so by activating Moonlight Act, her conditional manipulative Nen ability. Longhi explains that Moonlight Act allows her to loan her ability or to restrict the actions of those that sign a limited-term contract of hers. Longhi stresses that the signature must be voluntary, and asks Kurapika to hold off on agreeing or disagreeing to an alliance with Tubeppa in the contract before she finishes explaining her terms. 

Longhi reveals that Tubeppa is unaware of Longhi's ability, and unaware of her ability to use Nen in general. She explains that her contract is insurance to guarantee that her true plan goes smoothly. Kurapika, wary of lies, activates his Dowsing Chain and aims a gun at her, preparing to shoot her if she lies. Longhi asks why he bothers, pointing out that a Nen user cannot be killed with a .22 caliber handgun, but Kurapika does not respond. 

Seeing his lack of response, Longhi continues by sharing that her ability requires her to explain the whole truth first, and that telling Kurapika the details of a contract between him and Tubeppa would suffice if that were her only goal, but reveals that for her true plan, she wants to grant Kurapika additional power with her ability and has to explain everything about the true plan in order to do so. 

Longhi pauses, unsure of how to begin, but decides to start her explanation by saying what the previous true plan was, and reveals that she is Beyond Netero's daughter. Kurapika and Bill are visibly shocked, but Oito looks nervously in confusion. Longhi reveals that thirty years ago, Beyond talked some young Kakin officers into having sham marriages, and had the couples raise his children as their own and put into the Royal Military Academy, guessing that Beyond did this every time a new Prince was born. She shares that the children were set on the path to become Royal Guards for the princes and guesses that most of them have fulfilled the goal, because they had been Nen users since birth, and the exams they took were not particularly difficult. She says that she discovered the secret when she noticed a trait her "father" had that she didn't, and after finding out about the existence of her siblings simply guessed that Beyond's goal was to place his children into important positions near the next king. Longhi says that until the succession battle, she simply thought of herself as a Kakin soldier with unique family circumstances, not particularly close to her parents since she had been living in boarding schools as long as she could remember. She also says that she never questioned Beyond's plans, since they aligned with Kakin's national cause of world unity under Kakin. 

Longhi reveals that Beyond's means to achieve the cause were different. She shares that she was always physically among the top of her academy class without trying, because of her ability to use Nen since birth, but explains that it was a side effect of Beyond's Nen attack. Longhi shares that Beyond had placed a curse on his children, that she had discovered when her half sister Makaha became suspicious of her parents for not being as disappointed as she was when she was not chosen to become 2 nd Prince Camilla's personal guard. After the Seed Urn Ceremony, she and Makaha discovered a mark that both of them had, and learnt the truth when they visited a renowned exorcist. The wording allows for several interpretations but it is most likely that the intended meaning is that the mark existed before the Seed Urn ceremony, but was only discovered to be in common between Longhi and Makaha after. Although the official Viz translation uses the term "exorcist", the original Kanji used translates into "Nen user" and does not use the previously established term for "exorcist". 

Longhi asks Kurapika to use Gyo to take a look at the powerful and sinister Nen that had possessed her and her siblings, and opens her mouth to reveal an eye-like mark on the bottom of her tongue. Longhi shares that the exorcist had told her that she was a "strong curse sacrifice", among at least ten "strong curse sacrifices" chosen to curse their targets to death, possibly among even more "weak" ones that were not selected, and uses this to support her guess that Beyond prepared a large number of pawns because he could not be sure of the final count of Princes before the succession contest began. Longhi elaborates that she does not know what kind of ability it is, or who the targets are, but is firm in her confidence that the goal of the curse is to cause the death of their targets. 

Bill reminds Kurapika that taking too long would cause suspicion, but Kurapika decides to continue listening since they would not be able to form a contract with Tubeppa otherwise. Longhi continues explaining her theory, that since Beyond did not do anything in response to Makaha failing to join Camilla's entourage, their targets are not simply the princes that they were assigned to. Kurapika clarifies if despite not knowing who her target is, she is sure that it is a Prince, and Longhi confirms his understanding. Longhi points out that there are 14 Princes and at least 10 strong curse sacrifices, refusing to believe it is a coincidence, and shares that while she does not know who is going to kill which Prince, she is sure that Beyond is trying to influence the succession battle. 

Bill points out that the Beyond not telling the sacrifices about their status means that either Beyond has the activation method, or the activation is left to fate as part of a Nen limitation to increase the power of the curse. Kurapika guesses it is the latter, speculating that Beyond's goal is to help Benjamin. Longhi disagrees, saying that her father would not need have spent this much preparation time to support someone unrelated to him. Kurapika realizes the implication of Longhi's words—that Beyond has fathered one of the 14 Princes of Kakin, which Longhi confirms. Kurapika quickly asks Oito about the requirements for a Prince to participate in the succession battle, and confirms that the wordings allow for children born to Nasubi's legal wives but fathered by others to participate. 

A knock on the door comes from Sakata, who stands by the door with Hashito, guns at the ready, and asks Kurapika if he is ok. Kurapika steps out, and tells the participants that their negotiations are taking a while because they are unable to agree on the terms of the truce, promising to explain it fully afterwards. Furykov observes Kurapika, and notes that there is no change in Kurapika's aura, making him wonder what he was talking about with who he believed was Room 1014's assassin. 

Kurapika returns to Longhi, reactivates his Dowsing Chain, points his gun at Longhi, and asks her to continue. Longhi explains the details of the truce between Tubeppa, and Woble and Oito: 
Neither side will interfere with or attack the other side's Prince, Queen, or Guards 
This will last until 9 a.m. on Sunday (Day 15) 
The contract will automatically renew for another week, provided that a representative from Tubeppa is present in Room 1014 
Moonlight Act will activate only if Kurapika's side violates the agreed terms, and to enforce a week of Zetsu as penalty 
Healing or other acts of support are not counted as a Nen "attack" 
Tubeppa's side will not unilaterally terminate the contract as long as they do not uncover a malicious act from Woble's side towards Tubeppa's side 
Longhi will never arbitrarily interpret any of the actions of Woble's side as malicious 

Longhi adds another stipulation for her true plan: 
Woble's side must identify the Prince that is Beyond's child before either Tubeppa withdraws, or before only two Princes remain. 
If it is discovered that none of the Princes are Beyond's child, the ability would immediately activate and reward Woble's side 

Longhi shares that her additional stipulation will expire when Tubeppa cancels the truce, but if Kurapika is willing to continue the investigation. She promises to write him a new contract between him and her and compensate him in the same fashion, potentially rewarding him with a one-time use of Moonlight Act. Kurapika notes that Dowsing Chain has not indicated that Longhi is lying. Longhi asks Kurapika to listen to one final request before signing, and reveals her current true plan—should Beyond's child be among the Princes, to kill them herself. Longhi laments at Beyond's control of her, saying that she would have never made the decision to do so if Beyond had told her everything. 

Kurapika agrees to Longhi's contract, and agrees to collaborate with Tubeppa. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 The participants watch the three step out. Furykov notes that Longhi's Nen is tranquil and natural without signs of combat, and notices that she looks like she was crying, wondering what happened. Furykov feels something slightly different from before in Kurapika's aura, but is unable to discern if it's a change in himself or if it was Longhi's Nen. Furykov wonders if his deduction that Longhi was the assassin was incorrect. Kurapika invites Tenftory in and mentions that he would be the last to have his Nen awakened that day, saying that he would discuss the matter afterward. Tenftory later reports to 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei that all of the students who have taken Kurapika's Nen training are able to utilize Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

At 13:30 later that day, Tubeppa receives word from Maor and Longhi that Kurapika had entered the truce and agreed to their alliance, on condition that Woble and her mother Oito accompany him when he comes to Room 1005. Rihan listens while standing guard nearby. She tells them to, as much as they can, impress upon Benjamin's guards how close they are to Zhang Lei. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 

Abilities & Powers

Longhi underwent military training that she completed with ease thanks to her ability to use Nen from birth. 

Nen

Longhi is a Nen user. She is proficient enough at controlling the flow of her aura to successfully conceal her ability to use Nen from Kurapika, with Furykov, whose unique talent allowed him to unmask her right away, praising her skill. She has been able to use Ten and Zetsu since birth and is proficient enough in hatsu to have developed a Nen ability. She appears to be confident that a .22 caliber handgun would be unable to kill her, seemingly vouching for a certain degree of aptitude at Ken and, therefore, Ren. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 141 

Manga Appearances

Quotes

(To herself) "If... if only my father had told me everything... even if my role had been the same... I would have never... made this decision!" 

Trivia

Intertextuality and References

The subtitle Moonlight Act of Longhi's Transparent Words—Moonlight Act () is a reference to the manga series written and illustrated by Kazuhiro Fujita.
